tool_args.label: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service Spring 2015 Design Workshops Summary

payload.title: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service Spring 2015 Design Workshops Summary
payload.description: Summary of four design workshops held in April 2015 to update the community on the Woodhaven/Cross Bay Boulevard Q52/Q53 Select Bus Service project and gain feedback on proposed street designs and SBS bus stops

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYCDOT and the MTA held four design workshops
NYCDOT and the MTA held four design workshops

tool_args.label: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service

payload.project_name: Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevards Q52/53 Select Bus Service
payload.description: Select Bus Service project on Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards serving the Q52 and Q53 bus routes
Corridor for the Q52/53 Select Bus Service project spanning Woodhaven Boulevard and Cross Bay Boulevard

tool_args.label: Workshop 1: Woodhaven Blvd from Union Tpke to Rockaway Boulevard

payload.description: Design Workshop 1 covering Woodhaven Boulevard from Union Turnpike to Rockaway Boulevard. Held at PS 306 - NYC Academy for Discovery - Cafeteria, 96-16 89th Avenue, Woodhaven, NY 11421. 57 attendees.

tool_args.label: Workshop 2: Woodhaven Blvd from Queens Blvd to Union Tpke

payload.description: Design Workshop 2 covering Woodhaven Boulevard from Queens Boulevard to Union Turnpike. Held at Queens Metropolitan High School - Library, 91-30 Metropolitan Avenue, Forest Hills, NY 11366. 53 attendees.
Design Workshop 3 covering Cross Bay Boulevard. Held at PS 146 - The Howard Beach School - Cafeteria, 9801 189th Avenue, Howard Beach, NY 11414. 60 attendees.
Design Workshop 4 covering The Rockaways. Held at PS 42 - The Robert Vernam School - Science Classrooms, 488 Beach 66th Street, Arverne, NY 11692. 33 attendees.

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on transit service included: concern about SBS fare evasion; Q52/53 should operate as local buses after 11pm; off-board fare payment desired on local buses; transferring between SBS and local bus uses up free transfer; too many different bus routes on Woodhaven Blvd; need better frequency on all buses; consider a 'Super SBS' with no stops between Queens Blvd and the Rockaways; request for articulated buses; extend north end of Q52 to Woodside or LGA; off-board fare payment is a waste of paper, smart cards would be better; distance/zone pricing suggested; operate overnight service.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Transit service

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on bus lanes included: physical separation is necessary to keep general traffic out of bus lanes; bus lanes require effective enforcement; local buses may impede SBS buses; concern about too many buses in bus lanes; queuing on slip lanes may block bus lanes; consider two bus lanes in each direction (local and express); bus lane could also be an HOV lane; buses should be in the center of Woodhaven Blvd.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Bus lanes

tool_args.label: Community feedback on SBS bus stops

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on SBS bus stops included: when SBS bus stops are located between service and main roads, high barriers are needed to prevent unsafe crossing of the service road; need barriers high enough to prevent passengers from jumping over them; need to ensure ADA accessibility at SBS bus stops; need to ensure adequate lighting at median bus stops; the community should be informed about detailed design of median bus stops (fencing, shelters, etc) and proposed locations for physically separated bus lane.
payload.subject: Community feedback - SBS bus stops

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on safety included: request for improvements to help the elderly cross wide streets more safely; SBS bus stops on main road of Woodhaven Blvd will require riders to cross service road.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Safety

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on traffic included: left turn restrictions will divert more traffic onto side streets; specific concerns regarding left turn bans at Metropolitan, Myrtle, Jamaica, and Rockaway; slip lanes between main and service roads are needed with signage and advanced warning; signage needed for advance notice of turn restrictions; changing a general traffic lane to a bus-only lane will negatively impact all traffic; reducing general traffic lanes may make passage more difficult for emergency vehicles; need advance warning for left turn bans and alternate routes; school buses, trucks, and Access-A-Ride vehicles cause traffic backups on main road; sanitation trucks need to stop pick-ups during rush hour.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Traffic

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on parking included: curbside deliveries are an issue on Woodhaven Blvd; request not to remove any on-street parking.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Parking

payload.claim_text: Community feedback on community engagement included: community engagement should continue after SBS service begins to monitor and adjust; please include the map or proposed bus stops on the NYCDOT website; provide the full concept design online.
payload.subject: Community feedback - Community Engagement

payload.claim_text: Other community feedback: speed limit should be 25mph; green infrastructure would be beneficial to incorporate into project; bring back Long Island Rail Road (LIRR).
payload.subject: Community feedback - Other

tool_args.label: Focus area comments: Woodhaven Blvd Queens Blvd to Union Tpke

payload.claim_text: Focus area comments for Woodhaven Boulevard from Queens Boulevard to Union Turnpike included: support for Cooper Avenue as alternative for left turns at Metropolitan; concern about Trotting Course Lane becoming two-way; bus lanes should extend on bridge over LIRR; expand bridge over LIRR to accommodate bus lane; dangerous maneuver from eastbound Eliot Ave; need northbound slip lane from 65 Rd; northbound at Alderton service lane dead end should be safer merge; Wendy's bus stop at Metropolitan is unsafe; hot dog truck owner concerned about being displaced near World Gym; concern about traffic over bridge near Union Tpke; Metropolitan & Woodhaven is very dangerous; Metropolitan signal synchronization issue; 68 Rd has constant double parking; consider physical separation between bus lane and general traffic near Queens Blvd; potential problem with emergency vehicles merging to L.I.E. on-ramp.
payload.subject: Focus area: Queens Boulevard to Union Turnpike

tool_args.label: Focus area comments: Woodhaven Blvd Union Tpke to Rockaway/Liberty

payload.claim_text: Focus area comments for Woodhaven Boulevard from Union Turnpike to Rockaway Boulevard/Liberty Avenue included: skipping Atlantic on SBS is OK to improve speed; moving bus stop from Atlantic Avenue to 91st Street will inconvenience riders; move northbound bus stop at 81st Rd to near side of intersection; bus stop at 101 Ave is a good idea (connects to Q8); Park Lane South bus stop elimination requires difficult walk due to elevation; support for SBS stop at 101 Av; sidewalks need improvement between proposed 91 Av bus stop and Atlantic Avenue; proposed 101 Av turn restriction will improve safety for students crossing Woodhaven; need left turn on Jamaica Ave; left turn restrictions are better south of Rockaway Blvd because street grid offers more options; left turns at 85th/86th not seen as a fair trade for Jamaica left turn restriction; if you miss exit at Atlantic Av it would require too great a distance before you could turn left again; sidewalks need fixing, notably connecting 91 Av and Atlantic; 101 Av - 100 St need lighting under the overpass.
payload.subject: Focus area: Union Turnpike to Rockaway Boulevard/Liberty Avenue

tool_args.label: Focus area comments: Cross Bay Boulevard Rockaway to 165 Av

payload.claim_text: Focus area comments for Cross Bay Boulevard from Rockaway Boulevard to 165 Av included: support for new Pitkin Avenue bus stop; add Pitkin stop before SBS implementation; traffic often backs up near Pitkin Avenue; congestion along Cross Bay Blvd in Broad Channel area not too bad during weekday rush hours, very bad during summer months re: Rockaway Beach access; perceived higher percentage of car use on southern portion of corridor; Cross Bay Blvd congestion is a problem during summer months (for beach access); double parking is a problem on Cross Bay Blvd; proposed changes to Van Wicklen Road for a plaza should include public hearings; oppose left turn restriction at Rockaway Blvd; speeding problems on Cross Bay at connection to Belt Parkway; need left turns at 156th and 157th; Russo's Restaurant valet parking backs up past 163 Av south; Albert Rd needs a light; Conduit South currently has 5-6 cars doing left on red; need red light camera; need a one-bus trip from Howard Beach to Whitestone; Rockaway rail line re-established to alleviate traffic.
payload.subject: Focus area: Cross Bay Boulevard

payload.claim_text: Focus area comments for The Rockaways included: extend Q52 to Far Rockaway-Mott Av; possible extensions of Q52 in Rockaways starting at a depot at Beach 47th Street; try service extension on experimental basis; bus extension could aid the economy; request for Q52 expansion prior to capital project; extend to Sea Girt not Far Rockaway; extend Q53 to Breezy Point; extend Q22 to Breezy Point; project goals should articulate Rockaways-related goals; between 2pm and 5pm lots of buses needed because of schools; Beach 100 St pickup at 2:15pm and 3:04pm school buses (MTA); limit bike lanes - use space for bus lanes (Rockaways); only two ADA accessible subway stations in Rockaways; reduce unnecessary transfer by extending Q52 to Far Rockaway; strongly against eliminating bus stops on Q52 and in Broad Channel; transit customers tend to take Q52 or Q53 to Rockaway Blvd to transfer to A train; not opposed to SBS service but concerned City is not taking seriously the proposal for reactivation of LIRR Rockaway Line.
payload.subject: Focus area: The Rockaways
